title: viola sororia subsp. affinis
intro: Viola sororia subsp. affinis is a subspecies of the common blue violet (Viola sororia) plant, which is a perennial herb native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds, which have been found to be beneficial in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Viola sororia subsp. affinis include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies for its calming effects and potential therapeutic uses.
